title: Allionia ovata
intro: Allionia ovata, also known as Allionia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Allionia ovata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as isorhapontigenin and allionine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Allionia ovata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various conditions, including gastrointestinal disorders, skin problems, and respiratory issues.
